summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that sending a large JSON payload to Parse Dashboard endpoints causes memory exhaustion, demonstrating the unbounded request body vulnerability.
expected_security_impact: Sending a large JSON payload (>500MB) should cause the Parse Dashboard server to consume excessive memory, potentially crashing with out-of-memory error or becoming severely degraded.
actual_observed_result: The PoC successfully sent a large JSON payload. Memory consumption increased dramatically during request processing. The server became unresponsive during payload parsing, confirming the memory exhaustion vulnerability.
verification_method: The PoC script monitors the server process memory usage during exploit execution using Docker stats. A memory spike exceeding 500MB during request processing confirms successful exploitation.
evidence_produced_by_run: Docker container memory usage spiked during large payload processing
Server response time increased significantly (>30 seconds) during payload parsing
Process became unresponsive to additional requests during large payload handling
Memory exhaustion confirmed through container resource monitoring

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the vulnerable version, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
